examples.title: Use Case Template Structure
examples.description: Template showing the structure for writing a use case description
examples.code:
USE CASE: [Use Case Name]
ACTOR(S): [Primary Actor], [Secondary Actor]
PRECONDITION: [What must be true before starting]

BASIC FLOW:
1. [First step]
2. [Second step]
3. [Third step]
... 
n. [Final step - goal achieved]

POSTCONDITION: [What is guaranteed after completion]

ALTERNATIVE FLOW 1:
[Description of alternative scenario]
- [Step in alternative flow]
- [Return to basic flow or terminate]

ALTERNATIVE FLOW 2:
[Description of another alternative]
examples.explanation: This template provides a standardized format for writing use case descriptions. It ensures all important elements are included and makes it easier to compare different use cases.

examples.title: Use Case Diagram Elements (UML Notation)
examples.description: Pseudocode representation of UML use case diagram elements
examples.explanation: This pseudocode represents the UML notation for use case diagrams showing where elements are placed and how relationships are drawn.

examples.title: Example: ATM Withdraw Cash Flow
examples.description: Example use case showing basic and alternative flows for ATM withdrawal
examples.code:
USE CASE: Withdraw Cash
ACTOR(S): Customer (Primary), Bank System (Secondary)
PRECONDITION: Customer has valid ATM card, sufficient balance

BASIC FLOW:
1. Customer inserts ATM card
2. System validates card and requests PIN
3. Customer enters PIN
4. System validates PIN, displays menu
5. Customer selects "Withdraw Cash"
6. Customer enters amount
7. System verifies sufficient balance
8. System dispenses cash
9. System returns card
10. Customer takes cash

POSTCONDITION: Cash dispensed, account debited, transaction logged

ALTERNATIVE FLOW 1 (Invalid PIN):
3a. Customer enters incorrect PIN
3b. System displays error, allows retry
3c. Return to step 3 (max 3 attempts)

ALTERNITIVE FLOW 2 (Insufficient Balance):
6a. Customer enters amount exceeding balance
6b. System displays "Insufficient Funds"
6c. Return to step 6 or cancel

ALTERNATIVE FLOW 3 (Cancel):
4a. Customer selects "Cancel"
4b. System returns card
4c. Use case terminates
examples.explanation: This example demonstrates how to write a complete use case with basic flow and multiple alternative flows for handling exceptions.
